g.text: // High level is to design a webhook callback system
- Users can register a webhook URL to notify of them on certain events
- Internal services can queue these messages to trigger webhook calls
- guarantee at-least once delivery of all notifications
- notifications should be relatively low latency <1s after event trigger
- scalable to 1B events / day and 10k events / second and 1M organizations registered
- prioritize security for clients that can see the messages
- availability >> consistency (even if a server is offline, we can register and deliver webhook callbacks)
// External: Register a new webhook to be notified of certain events.
// Internal: services can send triggers for events
- guaranteed durability for at-least once delivery
- scale topics with more partitions if that organization
has an uneven amount of event triggers (bigger customer)
- ensure idempotency by hashing (organization_id, payload, event_type)
- use horizontal scaling by organization_id to
- ensure encryption via HMAC and registered security key
- maybe use Redis caching for faster reads per
- vertical scaling as needed based on queue depth
- persist this for transaction records to keep track which events were triggered
- organization should maintain secret_key to decrypt messages
- also pass in auth_token to ensure that correct sender
